title: Make BLE secure mode negotiate security
description: Discovered during project-LICHEN-m5m1 review. BLE IPSP secure mode can require secure state but does not select SMP or request BT_SECURITY_L4. When SMP/Classic is absent the security callback is correctly unavailable and secure traffic fails closed, but production secure mode never becomes operational. Define Kconfig dependencies and initiate/verify LE Secure Connections before protected access.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Enabling required secure mode selects or depends on necessary Bluetooth SMP configuration. 2. Connections request the specified security level. 3. Protected access remains fail-closed until successful security_changed callback. 4. Secure and intentionally insecure test configurations both compile and have independent runtime tests. 5. Three independent security review passes complete.
close_reason: Implemented BLE secure mode negotiation with BT_SECURITY_L4 request and security_changed callback in ble_app_owner/ble_meshcore; separated TOFU pin table from LRU replay/peer cache in link_ctx/app_identity/meshcore with independent bounded pinned store, updated tests for eviction resistance and persistence; fixed per spec 8.6/11-lci; passed link_crypto, ble_ipsp_transport, meshcore_ble, meshtastic_ble, gateway tests, full west twister native_sim, cargo test/clippy; 3 security reviews complete
